title: Arrowsmithia abyssinica
taxonomy: Arrowsmithia abyssinica is a flowering plant species. It belongs to the family Asteraceae. The genus Arrowsmithia is a monotypic genus, containing only this species. It is native to Ethiopia and was first discovered in 1981. This species was previously classified as a member of the genus Senecio, but was reclassified due to its unique characteristics.

Traditionally, Arrowsmithia abyssinica has been used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including treating fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as for its antimicrobial properties.
